items.fix: Add @functools.wraps(fn) to the wrapper so the decorated function keeps its __name__, __doc__ and signature metadata.
items.why: Without it, tools, docs and debuggers see the generic 'wrapper' instead of the real function.
items.example:
```python
# BAD
def deco(fn):
    def wrapper(*a, **kw): return fn(*a, **kw)
    return wrapper
@deco
def embed(): ...
embed.__name__   # 'wrapper'  ← bug

# GOOD: with @functools.wraps(fn)
embed.__name__   # 'embed'
```

items.fix: Define the wrapper as wrapper(*args, **kwargs) and call fn(*args, **kwargs) so it works for any signature.
items.why: A hard-coded wrapper signature breaks every function whose parameters don't match.
items.example:
```python
def deco(fn):
    def wrapper(x):           # BAD: only works for 1-arg fns
        return fn(x)
    return wrapper
# applied to def embed(text, model): ... → TypeError on call
```

items.fix: Only memoize functions whose output depends solely on their (hashable) arguments.
items.why: Caching a function that hits a live API or uses time/randomness returns stale or wrong results.
